text.title: django-constants Core Rules
text.summary: Five testable rules for centralised Django constants.

rule.statement: Every CharField/IntegerField with a fixed-value set MUST declare choices via a TextChoices or IntegerChoices subclass. Raw tuples like `choices=(('pending','Pending'),...)` are forbidden.
rule.rationale: TextChoices is hashable, IDE-completable, refactorable, and integrates with migrations. Raw tuples scatter string values across the codebase and resist rename tools.

rule.statement: Each app has a `constants.py` containing every TextChoices/IntegerChoices class and every business limit for that domain. models.py, services.py, and views.py import from it.
rule.rationale: Centralising constants gives one change location, one place for code review to scrutinise, and one import path for tests. Scattered constants make refactor and reasoning O(N).

rule.statement: Numeric business limits referenced in ≥ 2 files (max items, page size, retry count, rate limit) MUST be named UPPER_SNAKE_CASE module-level constants in constants.py. Numeric literals in services/selectors/models are forbidden when the value matches a known limit.
rule.rationale: UPPER_SNAKE signals "do not edit casually". One named source means a value change is one diff line; magic numbers require grep across the repo and hope.

rule.statement: Compare and assign enum members to other enum members or call-site values, NEVER to string literals. `status == OrderStatus.PENDING` is required; `status == "pending"` is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Enum-to-enum comparisons survive any rename of the underlying string. String comparisons hard-code the value at every call site and break silently on rename.

rule.statement: To display a label, use `OrderStatus.PENDING.label` directly. `dict(OrderStatus.choices).get(value)` is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Each TextChoices member has a `.label` attribute. Constructing a dict from `.choices` per call is wasteful and obscures intent.
